# Global Markets — Repo & Reverse Repo Standard Settlement Instructions The following instructions apply to all repo and reverse repo transactions executed under Global Master Repurchase Agreement (GMRA). Counterparties must confirm SSI details 24 hours prior to settlement date. ## Repo Settlement
CountryCorrespondent BankBICAccount NumberCPR RequiredCustodianSettlement Cycle

GERMANYDeutsche Bank AGDEUTDEFFDE89370400440532013000YesClearstream Banking FrankfurtT+2
FRANCEBNP Paribas SABNPAFRPPFR7630006000011234567890189YesEuroclear FranceT+2
NETHERLANDSSee GERMANY entry — same clearing path applies. Dutch securities settle via Clearstream Frankfurt using DEUTDEFF. Contact repo.ops@globalmarkets.internal for NL-specific ISIN queries.DEUTDEFFDE89370400440532013000YesClearstream Banking FrankfurtT+2
ITALYIntesa Sanpaolo SpABCITITMMIT60X0542811101000000123456YesMonte TitoliT+2

BELGIUMBNP Paribas Fortis SA/NVGEBABEBBBE68539007547034NoEuroclear BelgiumT+2

CANADARoyal Bank of CanadaROYCCAT2XXXCA789012345678YesCDS ClearingT+1
AUSTRALIAWestpac Banking CorporationWPACAU2SAU123456789NoAustraclearT+2
JAPANMUFG Bank LtdBOTKJPJTJP9876543210YesJASDECT+2
CPR (Counterparty Pre-notification Required): Where CPR is Yes, a SWIFT MT599 free-format message must be sent to the correspondent bank no later than 15:00 local time on the business day prior to settlement. For NETHERLANDS repo transactions, always reference the Germany SSI entry above. The same DEUTDEFF clearing path is used for all Dutch government bonds (DSL) and Dutch agency bonds settling via Clearstream Frankfurt. For ITALY repo on BTP (Buoni del Tesoro Poliennali), additional CONSOB reporting fields must be populated in the SWIFT message. Contact compliance@globalmarkets.internal before executing BTP repos above EUR 100 million. ## Triparty Repo Settlement
